mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-unemployment regimes exceed 1.5% in > 40% of cases despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and federal funds rates signal tightening monetary policy, which raises equity discount rates and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving inflation-rate regimes exceed 3% in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production indicates weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low-industrial-production regimes exceed 2% in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls reflects strong labor market demand, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-payroll-growth regimes fall below 0.5% in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals weak demand for construction, which reduces corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low-housing-starts regimes exceed 1.5% in > 35% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and industrial production indicates robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-expanding GDP-industry regimes fall below 1% in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.0% signals aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-rate regimes exceed 1.5% in > 40% of cases despite the signal.
